questions.questionText:
The value of $$\log \mathrm{K}$$ for the reaction $$\mathrm{A} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{B}$$ at $$298 \mathrm{~K}$$ is ___________. (Nearest integer)

Given: $$\Delta \mathrm{H}^{\circ}=-54.07 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$$

$$\Delta \mathrm{S}^{\circ}=10 \mathrm{~J} \mathrm{~K}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$$

(Take $$2.303 \times 8.314 \times 298=5705$$ )
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Let $$I(x)=\int \frac{x^{2}\left(x \sec ^{2} x+\tan x\right)}{(x \tan x+1)^{2}} d x$$. If $$I(0)=0$$, then $$I\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
If the system of equations

$$x+y+a z=b$$

$$2 x+5 y+2 z=6$$

$$x+2 y+3 z=3$$

has infinitely many solutions, then $$2 a+3 b$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The mean and variance of a set of 15 numbers are 12 and 14 respectively. The mean and variance of another set of 15 numbers are 14 and $$\sigma^{2}$$ respectively. If the variance of all the 30 numbers in the two sets is 13 , then $$\sigma^{2}$$ is equal to :

questions.questionText: Let $$\mathrm{A}=\left[\mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{ij}}\right]_{2 \times 2}$$, where $$\mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{ij}} \neq 0$$ for all $$\mathrm{i}, \mathrm{j}$$ and $$\mathrm{A}^{2}=\mathrm{I}$$. Let a be the sum of all diagonal elements of $$\mathrm{A}$$ and $$\mathrm{b}=|\mathrm{A}|$$. Then $$3 a^{2}+4 b^{2}$$ is equal to :

questions.questionText: Let $$\vec{a}=2 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}, \vec{b}=\hat{i}-2 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}$$ and $$\vec{c}=-\hat{i}+4 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}$$. If $$\vec{d}$$ is a vector perpendicular to both $$\vec{b}$$ and $$\vec{c}$$, and $$\vec{a} \cdot \vec{d}=18$$, then $$|\vec{a} \times \vec{d}|^{2}$$ is equal to :

$$B = \left\{ {x \in R:{3^x}{{\left( {\sum\limits_{r = 1}^\infty {{3 \over {{{10}^r}}}} } \right)}^{x - 3}} < {3^{ - 3x}}} \right\},$$ where [t] denotes greatest integer function. Then,

If $$\mathrm{m}_{1}$$ and $$\mathrm{m}_{2}$$ are the slopes of the lines $$\mathrm{l_{1}}$$ and $$\mathrm{l_{2}}$$, then the point of intersection of the line $$\mathrm{y=\left(m_{1}+m_{2}\right)}x$$ with L lies on :

Let $$a_{1}, a_{2}, a_{3}, \ldots, a_{\mathrm{n}}$$ be $$\mathrm{n}$$ positive consecutive terms of an arithmetic progression. If $$\mathrm{d} > 0$$ is its common difference, then

$$\lim_\limits{n \rightarrow \infty} \sqrt{\frac{d}{n}}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{a_{1}}+\sqrt{a_{2}}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{a_{2}}+\sqrt{a_{3}}}+\ldots \ldots \ldots+\frac{1}{\sqrt{a_{n-1}}+\sqrt{a_{n}}}\right)$$ is
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: One vertex of a rectangular parallelopiped is at the origin $$\mathrm{O}$$ and the lengths of its edges along $$x, y$$ and $$z$$ axes are $$3,4$$ and $$5$$ units respectively. Let $$\mathrm{P}$$ be the vertex $$(3,4,5)$$. Then the shortest distance between the diagonal OP and an edge parallel to $$\mathrm{z}$$ axis, not passing through $$\mathrm{O}$$ or $$\mathrm{P}$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$5 f(x)+4 f\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)=\frac{1}{x}+3, x > 0$$. Then $$18 \int_\limits{1}^{2} f(x) d x$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let the point $$(p, p+1)$$ lie inside the region $$E=\left\{(x, y): 3-x \leq y \leq \sqrt{9-x^{2}}, 0 \leq x \leq 3\right\}$$. If the set of all values of $$\mathrm{p}$$ is the interval $$(a, b)$$, then $$b^{2}+b-a^{2}$$ is equal to ___________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the ratio of the fifth term from the beginning to the fifth term from the end in the expansion of $$\left(\sqrt[4]{2}+\frac{1}{\sqrt[4]{3}}\right)^{\mathrm{n}}$$ is $$\sqrt{6}: 1$$, then the third term from the beginning is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$\mathrm{A}=\{1,2,3,4, \ldots ., 10\}$$ and $$\mathrm{B}=\{0,1,2,3,4\}$$. The number of elements in the relation $$R=\left\{(a, b) \in A \times A: 2(a-b)^{2}+3(a-b) \in B\right\}$$ is ___________.

questions.questionText: A circle passing through the point $$P(\alpha, \beta)$$ in the first quadrant touches the two coordinate axes at the points $$A$$ and $$B$$. The point $$P$$ is above the line $$A B$$. The point $$Q$$ on the line segment $$A B$$ is the foot of perpendicular from $$P$$ on $$A B$$. If $$P Q$$ is equal to 11 units, then the value of $$\alpha \beta$$ is ___________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: A long straight wire of circular cross-section (radius a) is carrying steady current I. The current I is uniformly distributed across this cross-section. The magnetic field is
options: (A) uniform in the region $$r < a$$ and inversely proportional to distance $$r$$ from the axis, in the region $$r > a$$
(B) zero in the region $$r < a$$ and inversely proportional to $$r$$ in the region $$r > a$$
(C) directly proportional to $$r$$ in the region $$r < a$$ and inversely proportional to $$r$$ in the region $$r > a$$
(D) inversely proportional to $$r$$ in the region $$r < a$$ and uniform throughout in the region $$r > a$$
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The number of air molecules per cm$$^3$$ increased from $$3\times10^{19}$$ to $$12\times10^{19}$$. The ratio of collision frequency of air molecules before and after the increase in number respectively is:
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Let $$y=y(x)$$ be a solution of the differential equation $$(x \cos x) d y+(x y \sin x+y \cos x-1) d x=0,0 < x < \frac{\pi}{2}$$. If $$\frac{\pi}{3} y\left(\frac{\pi}{3}\right)=\sqrt{3}$$, then $$\left|\frac{\pi}{6} y^{\prime \prime}\left(\frac{\pi}{6}\right)+2 y^{\prime}\left(\frac{\pi}{6}\right)\right|$$ is equal to ____________.

questions.questionText: Two resistances are given as $$\mathrm{R}_{1}=(10 \pm 0.5) \Omega$$ and $$\mathrm{R}_{2}=(15 \pm 0.5) \Omega$$. The percentage error in the measurement of equivalent resistance when they are connected in parallel is -

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R

Assertion A : When a body is projected at an angle $$45^{\circ}$$, it's range is maximum.

Reason R : For maximum range, the value of $$\sin 2 \theta$$ should be equal to one.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:
options: (A) Both $$\mathbf{A}$$ and $$\mathbf{R}$$ are correct and $$\mathbf{R}$$ is the correct explanation of $$\mathbf{A}$$
(D) Both $$\mathbf{A}$$ and $$\mathbf{R}$$ are correct but $$\mathbf{R}$$ is NOT the correct explanation of $$\mathbf{A}$$

options: (B) Both $$\mathbf{A}$$ and $$\mathbf{R}$$ are correct but $$\mathbf{R}$$ is NOT the correct explanation of $$\mathbf{A}$$

questions.questionText: A small block of mass $$100 \mathrm{~g}$$ is tied to a spring of spring constant $$7.5 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}$$ and length $$20 \mathrm{~cm}$$. If the block moves in a circular path on a smooth horizontal surface with constant angular velocity $$5 ~\mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}$$ about point $$\mathrm{A}$$, then tension in the spring is -

Let $$\mathrm{C}_{1}$$ and $$\mathrm{C}_{2}$$ be the capacitance of the system for $$\chi=\frac{1}{3} d$$ and $$\mathcal{X}=\frac{2 d}{3}$$, respectively. If $$\mathrm{C}_{1}=2 \mu \mathrm{F}$$ the value of $$\mathrm{C}_{2}$$ is __________ $$\mu \mathrm{F}$$

questions.questionText: A pole is vertically submerged in swimming pool, such that it gives a length of shadow $$2.15 \mathrm{~m}$$ within water when sunlight is incident at angle of $$30^{\circ}$$ with the surface of water. questions.questionText: Let $$a \in \mathbb{Z}$$ and $$[\mathrm{t}]$$ be the greatest integer $$\leq \mathrm{t}$$. Then the number of points, where the function $$f(x)=[a+13 \sin x], x \in(0, \pi)$$ is not differentiable, is __________.

For the adsorption of hydrogen on platinum, the activation energy is $$30 ~\mathrm{k} ~\mathrm{J} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}$$ and for the adsorption of hydrogen on nickel, the activation energy is $$41.4 ~\mathrm{k} ~\mathrm{J} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}$$. The logarithm of the ratio of the rates of chemisorption on equal areas of the metals at $$300 \mathrm{~K}$$ is __________ (Nearest integer)

Given: $$\ln 10=2.3$$

$$\mathrm{R}=8.3 \mathrm{~J} \mathrm{~K}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$$
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements, one is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R.

Assertion A : Loss of electron from hydrogen atom results in nucleus of $$\sim 1.5 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{pm}$$ size.

Reason R : Proton $$\left(\mathrm{H}^{+}\right)$$ always exists in combined form.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
options: (A) Both $$\mathbf{A}$$ and $$\mathbf{R}$$ are correct and $$\mathbf{R}$$ is the correct explanation of $$\mathbf{A}$$
(B) A is correct but R is not correct
(C) A is not correct but R is correct
(D) Both $$\mathbf{A}$$ and $$\mathbf{R}$$ are correct but $$\mathbf{R}$$ is NOT the correct explanation of $$\mathbf{A}$$
questions.subject: Chemistry
